stem: In a right triangle, the tangent of one of the two acute angles is √5/2. What is the tangent of the other acute angle?
rationale: In a right triangle, the two acute angles are complementary, so the tangent of one equals the reciprocal of the tangent of the other (since tan(90°−θ) = cot θ = 1/tan θ). Therefore the other tangent is 2/√5. Rationalizing: 2/√5 = 2√5/5. Choice A repeats the original value; choice B is the reciprocal in unrationalized form (also equal to c, but not in rationalized form); choice D is the squared reciprocal. The standard rationalized answer is 2√5/5.
